auth_notes: Requires a Client ID and Client Secret to generate a Bearer Token via a POST request to the identity endpoint. Tokens usually expire in 3600 seconds and must be refreshed programmatically within the workflow.
rate_limits: Typically 50 requests per second for production environments, though varies by specific contract and clearinghouse.
gotchas: Eligibility checks (270/271) are synchronous but Claim submissions (837) often require polling for status updates.
Data must strictly adhere to HIPAA-compliant EDI X12 standards, even when using JSON-to-EDI translation layers.
Payer IDs are not universal and must be mapped specifically to the clearinghouse being used.
client_must_provide: Payer ID list for target insurance carriers
Business Associate Agreement (BAA) for HIPAA compliance
research_notes: Implementation requires an HTTP Request node to handle the OAuth2 handshake. A second HTTP Request node will then POST the patient/provider data to the eligibility endpoint. Since n8n does not have a native EDI parser, the developer should use the REST/JSON wrappers provided by the clearinghouse rather than raw X12 to simplify integration.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Implementation requires an HTTP Request node to handle the OAuth2 handshake. A second HTTP Request node will then POST the patient/provider data to the eligibility endpoint. Since n8n does not have a native EDI parser, the developer should use the REST/JSON wrappers provided by the clearinghouse rather than raw X12 to simplify integration.
